nodes.title: Cell Biology
nodes.description: Advanced survey of cell organelles, their composition, origin, and the relationship between their structure and function. Emphasis on recent literature and current controversies. Prerequisites: CHEM 2585; Biology Graduate student; or Instructor permission.

nodes.title: Critical Reading & Analysis
nodes.description: Runs concurrently with CLBI 6010 and utilizes primary literature and an active, discussion-based approach to provide intensive study in the logic, critical thinking, and experimental design & interpretation. Co-requisite: CLBI 6010.

nodes.title: Biomedical Data Analysis
nodes.description: Introduction to qualitative, quantitative and statistical analysis for cell, molecular, and biomedical sciences. The practical philosophy underlying data presentation and interpretation will be emphasized via problem solving in and outside of class time.

nodes.title: Organic Chemistry 2
nodes.description: Reactivity of organic compounds and applications to synthesis. Spectroscopy is discussed in relation to compound characterization. For premedical and biological sciences students. Credit not awarded for both CHEM 2585 and CHEM 1550. Prerequisite: CHEM 1500 or CHEM 2580.
nodes.other_requirements: Must register for a CHEM 2585 lab, L01-L20; Degree students only even after level restrictions removed; Post Bac Pre Med students register for CHEM 2585 ZRA; Fee: $85

nodes.title: Science Communication
nodes.description: Develop effective oral and written communication skills for a range of audiences from academia to industry, organizations, news, policymakers, and the general public.

nodes.title: IS:Machine Lrng in Immunology
nodes.description: A course which is tailored to fit the interests of a specific student, which occurs outside the traditional classroom/laboratory setting under the supervision of a faculty member, for which credit is awarded. Offered at department discretion.
